hyperedges.label: PLoRA + ASHA + RsLoRA HP Sweep Stack
Three-generation ASR fairness benchmark (CTC / Whisper / LLM-decoder)
Phase 3 SFT LoRA pipeline (infra + VRAM tune + launch)
Head Surgery diagnosis pipeline (Stages A-F + fixing set)
Data loader stack (dataset + sampler + factory + splits)

hyperedges.description: Stage 1 (20 trials, validate packing) -> Stage 2 (100 trials, +ASHA, best 0.1424 rank=4) -> Stage 3 (100 trials, +RsLoRA, best 0.1451 rank=8)
Baseline -> full 640-cell sweep -> per-head scoring with regression guard
Same matrix solved two ways: greedy (approximation) + ILP (optimum) for comparison
All three filters must hold for a head to be valid in the fixing set
Three bottleneck categories in ML training diagnosis
SFT expands -> RL squeezes -> Rejection sampling feeds next SFT round
First three VRAM techniques applied in order per the playbook
CPU decode ceiling is the root cause; Option 1 fixes it, Option 4 is cheap default
Two audio fixes collapsed 9.62% -> 1.27%, triggering the null-result pivot
Three canonical sources synthesized into ml-prototyping guide
Top 3 LoRA HP priority: LR > target_modules > rank

nodes.label: Option 2: tmpfs Staging (ruled out)
nodes.description: Caches bytes not decoded tensors; ruled out after pre-flight

nodes.label: Option 3: Warm Page Cache (ruled out)
nodes.description: Same reason as Option 2; ~0 pp measured

nodes.label: Option 4: num_workers=2
nodes.description: +4.7 pp util, -0.16 s/step; fewer workers reduce 4-core thrash

nodes.label: Option 5: Migrate to 8+ core NVMe host
nodes.description: 1.5-2x per ~2x CPU cores; proportional to decode parallelism
